title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLIV. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
Old woman, old woman, shall we go a shearing?
        Speak a little louder, sir, I am very thick of hearing.
        Old woman, old woman, shall I love you dearly?
        Thank you, kind sir, I hear you very clearly.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCLIV. Gaffers And Gammers.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
